result.markdown: --- title: "" --- ## Week 2 ### Day 1 #### [Today]{style="color:red;"} - Lecture: Probability - Lecture Code: Probability.R ##### **In Class Discussion Questions for Betts et al. 2022** - Is the use of hypotheses in the literature changing? - When are hypotheses useful? - When are hypotheses not useful? ##### **In Class Discussion Questions for Dumelle et al. 2021 and Williams & Brown 2019** - What are the attributes and foundations of design-based sampling/inference? - What are the attributes and foundations of model-based sampling/inference? - How do you use these two ideas? - Any concerns of using or not using these ideas? #### [Assigned]{style="color:red;"} - [Probabiltiy Quiz on Canvas](https://colostate.instructure.com/courses/193678/quizzes) - Readings for next class: - Kery and Kellner Chapter 2 (sections 2.0-2.4) - Background Reading (not required): - Hobbs & Hooten Ch. 3. Section 3.4. - Bolker 2007 Ch. 4. ### Day 2 (lab) #### [Today]{style="color:red;"} - Lecture: Continue with Probability - Downloads: Files #### [Assigned]{style="color:red;"} - Readings for next class: - Kery and Kellner Chapter 2 (sections 2.5-2.6) - Dushoff et al. 2019 - Fieberg, Chapter 1.10 - Background Reading on the Likelihood and Optimization (not required): - [Bolker Ch. 6](/readings/Bolker 2007 Chapter 6.pdf) - [Bolker Ch. 7](/readings/Bolker 2007 Chapter 7.pdf) - [Fieberg Ch. 10](https://statistics4ecologists-v3.netlify.app/10-maximumlikelihood) - Background Reading on regression (not required): - [Fieberg, Ch. 1](https://statistics4ecologists-v2.netlify.app/linreg) - [Zurr Ch. 2](/readings/Zurr et al Chapter 2.pdf) #### [Using Code from Stats4Ecologists]{style="color:red;"} ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # You will need to install this package: remotes::install_github("jfieberg/Data4Ecologists") ``` :::
